name: Atari Sunset
description: Red sky, mountains, stars — glitched like a cartridge pulled too soon

name: Phosphor Horizon
description: Purple phosphor glow across distant mountains — the monitor at 2am

name: Commodore Dawn
description: Blue ocean light, clouds on the horizon — a new load begins

name: Ember & Ash
description: Orange afterburn, clouded sky — the world after the render completes

name: Perfect Sunset
description: Warm sunset palette with mountains and soft clouds — no glitches here

name: Expo '67 Twilight
description: Retro World's Fair skyline at golden dusk — pavilions against a bruised sky

name: World of Tomorrow
description: Futurist city at dusk — the 1939 World's Fair imagined from the year 2099

name: Text Adventure City
description: Retro night city, dense as a dungeon level — lit windows suggest treasure

name: Neon Dystopia
description: Futuristic high-density skyline at night — rain on the sensor array

name: Commodore Zodiac
description: 12 invented stars with ancient-sounding machine lore — Sector 64 mythology

description: 9 Norse stars — the number of worlds, mapped overhead

name: Algorithm's Heaven
description: 16 invented stars with no lore — just the diagram of a forgotten proof

name: Colors in Conflict
description: Mondrian grid at maximum complexity — primary colors refusing to share space

name: Sierpinski Weekend
description: Recursive monochrome — the triangle inside the triangle inside the triangle

name: Sea Glass Harmony
description: Woven teal forms at low complexity — colors that agree with each other

name: Circuit Mind
description: Circuit-trace geometry in monochrome — the board as abstract painting

name: Forest Weave
description: Interlocking forest-palette forms — generative art that grew from a seed

name: Neon Logic
description: Three inputs, XOR/NOR/AND gates, neon style — the circuit dreams in light

name: Blueprint Classic
description: Three inputs on paper — AND and OR on a blueprint from the file cabinet

name: Binary Confusion
description: Four inputs, three levels deep, NAND/NOR/XOR — the logic of forgetting

name: Time Out of Joint
description: Three haiku about the moment before the next moment arrives

params.theme: the moment between seconds, time folded back on itself

name: Last Save
description: Three epitaphs for things that ran out of memory

params.theme: the final cartridge, the last checkpoint, the save that did not take

name: BASIC Meditation
description: Two lore fragments from the manual of a machine that never shipped

params.theme: the machine dreams in phosphor, BASIC programs that loop forever

description: Four couplets built from words that sound alike but mean otherwise

name: World's Fair Elegy
description: Three lore fragments from pavilions that no longer exist

description: 5 shades of monitor glow — the dark basement, the lit screen

description: 8 shades from the Commodore 64's fixed palette — 1982 calling

name: Mandelbrot SVG
description: An SVG visualization of the Mandelbrot set with escape-time coloring

params.freeform: Generate an SVG visualization of the Mandelbrot set. Use escape-time coloring with a fractal boundary. Output a complete SVG with viewBox="0 0 800 600".

name: Time Capsule 1964
description: A time capsule artifact from the 1964 World's Fair — as SVG

params.freeform: Generate an SVG of a time capsule document from the 1964 New York World's Fair. Include a decorative border, a message to the future, and futurist typography. Output a complete SVG with viewBox="0 0 600 800".

name: Recursive Poem
description: A poem that contains itself — or tries to

params.freeform: Write a short poem that references itself as an artifact — a poem that knows it is a poem being generated, and folds that awareness into its form. Plain text output.

name: BASIC Koan
description: A Zen koan written in BASIC — 10 PRINT is the teacher

params.freeform: Write a Zen koan in the style of a BASIC program listing. Line numbers, PRINT statements, GOTO loops. The koan should be about the nature of iteration, memory, or forgetting. Plain text output.
